title: Performance
description: Review Angular performance considerations, change detection, and when to optimize templates and observations.
checklist: Explain how change detection impacts Angular performance
Describe when to use OnPush change detection
Identify expensive template expressions to avoid
Summarize lazy loading and module splitting benefits
Mention the cost of large watch lists or observable subscriptions
answerHtml:
Sub QuestionAnswerMore ExplanationExampleBenefit
How does change detection affect performance?More checks mean more workAngular inspects bindings on each change detection cycleLarge component trees run slowerUnderstand render cost
When use OnPush?For immutable inputs and fewer checksComponent only checks when inputs change or events occur@Component({ changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush })Reduces unnecessary cycles
What are expensive template expressions?Functions or complex logic inside templatesThey run on every change detection pass{{ computeValue(item) }}Prefer pipes and memoized values
How does lazy loading help?Load modules only when neededReduces initial bundle size and startup cost{ path: 'admin', loadChildren: () => import('./admin/admin.module') }Faster initial load
Why limit subscriptions and watchers?Each one adds runtime overheadUnmanaged subscriptions can leak and slow the apptakeUntil(destroy$)More stable, efficient code
